pages.raw_text:
The land office in the Hutton
building was opened to filers on
timber and agricultural land on the
Priest River reserve at 8 o'clock
this morning. There followed nc
rush, due TO the orderly arrange
ment made yesterday when 15C
prospective settlers formed in line


Up to the noon hour As people
had filed. OF this number 40 took
choice timber and stone claims, and
5 others homesteads. AS early as
10 o'clock yesterday morning the
settlers began to gather. At IL
o'clock last night the line filled the
Hutton building and the doors were
locked and the people compelled to
60 out doors. Patrolman Joe Dan
iels, who had been on duty during
the day, was asked to keep order
by the settlers themselves, who re.
allied that with such a mob, sew.
eral trying to obtain the same
ground, disorder was bound TO fol
low. Daniels was equal to the oc-
casion and passed out numbers and
formed the people in line on the
sidewalk. He assessed each person
25 cents. There were $50 of them,
which made Daniels $3.50 for his
nights work. Was this graft or
what?


They were sorry looking crowd
this morning. Most of them slept
in their places on the line. When
ever / had occasion to go for re-
cess or get something to eat, the
patrolman would be notified and
when he returned he presented his
number and was once more allowed
his place. In this manner perfect
discipline prevailed all night and
this morning everything went
smoothly. There are probably %
women In the crowd. They stuck
